literature-report-plan.name: R4-multiphoton-neuroscience

metadata.title: Multiphoton Microscopy for Neuroscience: Smart Acquisition and Closed-Loop Feedback

literature-report-plan.invocation-prompt:
create literature report on "Multiphoton Microscopy for Neuroscience: Smart Acquisition and Closed-Loop Feedback"
Focus: Recent trends in fundamental neuroSCIENCE (NOT clinical applications) related to our data acquisition flow:
A) SMART ACQUISITION: Real-time algorithms for 2-photon microscopy. Adaptive scanning strategies. How to decide "when enough data has been collected." Dose reduction via intelligent field selection (Ye et al. 16x reduction).
B) CLOSED-LOOP FEEDBACK: Papers using closed-loop neural feedback with in vivo studies in mice and rats. Real-time decoding during acquisition. Neurofeedback modulation. How segmentation quality feeds back into acquisition decisions.
C) DATA INFRASTRUCTURE: Cloud-based neuroscience data analysis. OME-Zarr for large volumetric data. Compression strategies for electrophysiology and imaging. Reproducible analysis pipelines (SpikeInterface, Suite2p).
Context: Our repo has a data acquisition flow (Flow 0) that currently just downloads datasets. The future vision (Flow 0b) is an active acquisition agent that guides 2-photon microscope operators based on segmentation uncertainty. This report provides the neuroscience context.
Key existing ref: docs/planning/flow-data-acquisition-plan.md
EXCLUDED topics: - Clinical medical imaging (report R2/R3) - MLOps infrastructure (report R2) - Computational reproducibility (report R1) - Agentic AI architecture (report 5)
Repo context: /home/petteri/Dropbox/github-personal/minivess-mlops KG domains to update: data, models Manuscript section: Discussion (smart acquisition, future of 2-PM + ML integration)

seed-papers.paper: Coulter et al. (2025). "Closed-Loop Modulation of Remote Hippocampal Representations with Neurofeedback." Neuron 113(6). https://doi.org/10.1016/j.neuron.2024.12.023
Fang et al. (2025). "Advancements in Neural Closed-Loop Manipulations in Awake, Behaving Animals." Current Opinion in Behavioral Sciences 66. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cobeha.2025.101597
Chu et al. (2025). "RealtimeDecoder: Fast Software Module for Online Clusterless Decoding." eNeuro 12(12). https://doi.org/10.1523/ENEURO.0252-24.2025
Bright et al. (2026). "AQuA2-Cloud: A Web Platform for Fluorescence Bioimaging Activity Analysis." bioRxiv. https://doi.org/10.64898/2026.03.06.709938
Buccino et al. (2025). "Efficient and Reproducible Pipelines for Spike Sorting." bioRxiv. https://doi.org/10.1101/2025.11.12.687966
Lee et al. (2025). "Compression Benchmarking of Holotomography Data Using OME-Zarr." arXiv. https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2503.18037
Yamane et al. (2025). "Optical Neuroimage Studio (OptiNiSt)." PLOS Computational Biology. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pcbi.1013087
Kim et al. (2025). "The Future of Neurotechnology." Neuron 113(6). https://doi.org/10.1016/j.neuron.2025.02.019
Poon et al. (2023). "A dataset of rodent cerebrovasculature." Scientific Data 10, 141. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41597-023-02048-8
Evangelou et al. (2025). "EthoPy: Reproducible Behavioral Neuroscience Made Simple." bioRxiv. https://doi.org/10.1101/2025.09.08.673974
Lakunina et al. (2025). "Neuropixels Opto." bioRxiv. https://doi.org/10.1101/2025.02.04.636286

search-domains.domain: closed-loop neurofeedback mouse rat in vivo 2024 2025
multiphoton imaging vascular neuroscience analysis pipeline 2024
online decoding calcium imaging real-time processing 2025
